---
title: "4.13 Sum and Max 2D Array Algorithms"
subtitle: "Implementing 2D Array Algorithms"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Implementing 2D Array Algorithms

### Standard 2D traversal algorithms

All of the array algorithms can be applied to 2D arrays too.

There are standard algorithms that utilize 2D array traversals to:

- determine a minimum or maximum value of all elements or a designated row, column, or subsection
- compute a sum or average of all elements or a designated row, column, or subsection
- determine if at least one element has a particular property
- determine if all elements have a particular property
- determine the number of elements having a particular property

## More 2D Algorithms

### Pairs, duplicates, shifts, rotations

Standard algorithms also include:

- access all consecutive pairs of elements
- determine the presence or absence of duplicate elements
- shift or rotate elements in a row left or right or in a column up or down
- reverse the order of the elements in a row or column

## From 1D to 2D

### Use nested loops

With 1D arrays, many algorithms followed patterns with a `for` loop or enhanced `for` loop.

With 2D arrays, you need nested loops to traverse rows and columns.

Indexed `for` loops are often used to better control the row and column index values.

Enhanced `for` loops can also be used.

## 2D Traversal Patterns

### Indexed and enhanced loops

```java
// 2 Dimensional Array Traversal
for (int row = 0; row < array.length; row++)
{
    for (int col = 0; col < array[0].length; col++)
    {
        if (condition about array[row][col])
        {
            // body for matching values
        }
    }
}

// enhanced for loops
for (int[] row : array)
{
    for (int value : row)
    {
        if (condition about value)
        {
            // body for matching values
        }
    }
}
```

## Sum, Average, Min, Max

### Row and column totals

Counting and searching algorithms work very similarly in 1D and 2D arrays.

The following source code adds all of the values in a given row and asks you to complete a method that totals a column.

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: lca2dloopPart`

What will the following code print out?

Complete the method called `getTotalForCol` that gets the total for a column.

To do this, loop through the rows.

The array's length tells you how many rows you have, while the length of the array's first element tells you how many columns.

## Total Starter

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class Total
{

    public static int getTotalForRow(int row, int[][] a)
    {
        int total = 0;
        for (int col = 0; col < a[0].length; col++)
        {
            total = total + a[row][col];
        }
        return total;
    }

    // Complete the method getTotalForCol below
    public static int getTotalForCol(int col, int[][] a)
    {
        int total = 0;
        // Add a loop here to total a column col

        return total;
    }

    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        int[][] matrix = { {1, 2, 3}, {4, 5, 6}};
        System.out.println(getTotalForRow(0, matrix));
        System.out.println(getTotalForCol(0, matrix));
    }
}
```
:::

## Total Test Targets

### Runestone checks

The output from `main` should be:

```text
6
5
```

A second test calls `getTotalForCol(0, { {1, 4, 8}, {6, 7, 9} })`.

Expected return value:

```text
7
```

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: max2DArrayParsons`

The following has the correct code to find the largest value in a 2D array.

Drag the blocks into the correct order and indent them.

::: {.code-scroll .compact style="max-height: 360px;"}
```java
public int getLargest(int[][] arr)
{
=====
 int largest = arr[0][0];
 int current = 0;
 for (int r = 0; r < arr.length; r++)
 {
=====
   for (int c = 0; c < arr[0].length; c++)
   {
=====
     current = arr[r][c];
     if (current > largest)
     {
=====
       largest = current;
=====
     } // end if
=====
   } // end column loop
=====
 } // end row loop
 return largest;
=====
} // end method
```
:::

## Mixed-Up Code: Correct Order

### max2DArrayParsons

```java
public int getLargest(int[][] arr)
{
 int largest = arr[0][0];
 int current = 0;
 for (int r = 0; r < arr.length; r++)
 {
   for (int c = 0; c < arr[0].length; c++)
   {
     current = arr[r][c];
     if (current > largest)
     {
       largest = current;
     } // end if
   } // end column loop
 } // end row loop
 return largest;
} // end method
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

- translate 1D algorithms into nested 2D traversals
- use `array.length` for rows and `array[0].length` for columns
- total a row by looping over columns
- total a column by looping over rows
- initialize max from `arr[0][0]` before scanning the whole 2D array

## End

### 4.13 Sum and Max 2D Array Algorithms

Next: subsection loops and duplicate checks.
